vilka-ar-vi.metaDescription: Scouting gives children and young people opportunities for adventure and personal growth in an inclusive community where diversity, respect, and cooperation are at the heart of everything we do.
vilka-ar-vi.title: About HSS
vilka-ar-vi.subTitle: Established in 1959

1.text: Hässelby Strands Sjöscoutkår (HSS) is a traditional non-profit youth organization with a focus on scouting and boating. We are religiously and politically independent, and a member of the Svenska Scoutförbundet (Swedish Scout Association), which with about 65,000 members is one of Sweden's largest youth organizations.

2.text: The goal of our activities is to give children and young people the opportunity to experience nature, boating, seamanship, challenges and above all camaraderie. The basic idea is Learning by doing. There is no substitute bench in Scouterna!

3.text: HSS was formed in 1959 and operates in a pleasant lodge (called Ruffen) on the shores of Lake Mälaren in Hässelby. HSS has around 130 happy members, owns five sailboats, ten optimist dinghies, two 2-krona boats, a fast escort boat and a large scout cabin.

1.text: HSS was founded in 1959 and grew rapidly thanks to the strong commitment of leaders, parents and scouts. Together they helped build the business through activities, fundraising and volunteer work.

2.text: The corps' first boat, the Igelknäcken (“Inga-Lunda”), was the start of HSS's strong sea scout tradition. Over time, more boats could be purchased thanks to bazaars, lotteries and a great deal of joint commitment. During the 1960s, the corps continued to grow. More children and young people found their way into scouting life, and sailing on Lake Mälaren became a natural part of the activities. New boats were launched and sea life became an important part of the community.

3.text: The need for a gathering place on land also emerged. Through many voluntary efforts, the Myset scout hut was built, which was inaugurated in 1968. It is still used today as a place for camps, community and memories.

4.text: Since its inception, HSS has been a sea scout corps characterized by community, cooperation and adventure. It is a tradition that still lives on today.
